metadata.description: A beginner Mandarin starter pack built around real-life sentence islands, pinyin with tone marks, hanzi, literal translations, word breakdowns, sentence structure notes, usage notes, and reusable grammar patterns.

appInstructions.sourceOfTruth: This JSON file should drive the UI. Do not hardcode sentence content in app components.
flashcardFlow: User attempts to say Mandarin aloud.
Reveal hanzi, pinyin, literal meaning, structure notes, and breakdown.
Persist mastery in localStorage by sentence id.
audioInstructions: Use SpeechSynthesis with zh-CN for MVP if available.
Speak the hanzi field, not pinyin.
Allow shadowing mode where user hears Mandarin then repeats aloud.
audioUrl is reserved for future real audio files.

islands.name: Food / Cooking
islands.goal: Eating, cooking, groceries, and taste feedback.

islands.name: Morning / Night Routine
islands.goal: Common waking up, sleeping, tiredness, and household rhythm.

islands.name: Home / Daily Life
islands.goal: Basic house, chores, objects, and daily actions.

islands.name: Work / Programming
islands.goal: Workday, meetings, code, bugs, and tech complaints.

islands.name: Opinions / Personality
islands.goal: Sound like yourself: opinions, disagreement, curiosity, jokes, reactions.

islands.name: Conversation Control
islands.goal: Ask for repetition, clarification, slower speech, and help.

islands.name: Shopping / Errands
islands.goal: Stores, buying things, needing things, and basic errands.

islands.name: Time / Plans
islands.goal: Today, tomorrow, later, soon, before/after, and simple plans.

islands.name: Survival Mandarin
islands.goal: High-value beginner phrases for real-world interaction.

patterns.name: Subject + Verb + Object
patterns.meaning: Basic sentence order for many Mandarin sentences.
patterns.notes: Often similar to English. Pronouns do not change form like I/me/my in English.

patterns.name: Yes/No Question With 吗
patterns.meaning: Turns many statements into yes/no questions.
patterns.notes: Do not use 吗 with question words like 什么 or 哪里.

patterns.name: Change Of State With 了
patterns.meaning: Shows something has become true or changed state.
patterns.notes: At beginner level, learn it in fixed phrases like 我饿了, 我累了, 我困了.

patterns.name: 我要 + Action
patterns.meaning: I want to / I am going to.
patterns.notes: 要 can mean want, need, or intend depending on context.

patterns.name: 我想 + Action
patterns.meaning: I want to / I feel like / I would like to.
patterns.notes: Softer than 要. Also means miss when used with a person, as in 我想你了.

patterns.name: 你想 + Action + 什么
patterns.notes: High-value pattern for food, watching, buying, doing, and going.

patterns.name: 很 + Adjective
patterns.notes: 很 often acts as a natural linker before adjectives. It does not always mean very strongly.

patterns.name: Verb + 一下
patterns.meaning: Do an action briefly / once / for a moment.
patterns.notes: Makes requests sound more casual and softer.

patterns.name: Possession With 的
patterns.meaning: Owner's thing / my / your / his / her.

patterns.name: Can You With 可以
patterns.meaning: Can you / may you do something?

patterns.name: Let Me With 让我
patterns.notes: Common in phrases like 让我看看 and 让我想一下.

patterns.name: Too Adjective With 太...了
patterns.notes: Common for reactions: 太辣了, 太贵了, 太好了.

patterns.name: Negation With 不
patterns.notes: Common negation. 不 changes tone before fourth tone in speech, but pinyin may still be written as bù.

patterns.name: Do Not Have / There Is Not With 没有
patterns.meaning: Do not have / there is not / did not.
patterns.notes: Useful beginner phrase: 没有 = don't have / no.

sentences.structureNotes: Simple Subject + Verb + Object sentence.
sentences.usageNotes: Direct and common. Romantic, serious, or affectionate depending on context.

sentences.structureNotes: 想 + person means miss that person. 了 makes it feel like 'I miss you now.'
sentences.usageNotes: Natural affectionate phrase for a partner.

sentences.structureNotes: A short directional phrase. No subject is needed in casual commands.
sentences.usageNotes: Casual. Tone matters; can sound affectionate or bossy depending on delivery.

sentences.literal: Give me kiss one brief action.
sentences.structureNotes: 给我 means give me. 亲一下 softens the action into 'kiss me once / give a quick kiss.'
sentences.usageNotes: More natural and casual than 给我亲吻. Between couples, 亲我一下 is also natural.

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + person + 一下 can make an affectionate request.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural with a partner or close family.

sentences.structureNotes: Mandarin often uses 很 before adjectives where English uses 'is/are.'
sentences.usageNotes: Natural compliment. Do not say 你是可爱 in normal speech.

sentences.structureNotes: Subject + 很 + adjective.
sentences.usageNotes: Can be playful or rude depending on tone and relationship.

sentences.structureNotes: This is a common fixed phrase. Treat it as one chunk at first.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful when teasing someone or clarifying that you were not serious.

examples.pinyin: bié shēng qì, wǒ kāi wán xiào de
examples.english: Don't be mad, I am joking.

sentences.structureNotes: Statement + 了吗 asks if a state has become true.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful but potentially sensitive. Tone matters.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed everyday phrase. Learn as one chunk.
sentences.usageNotes: Very common when someone seems upset or something happened.

examples.english: What's wrong with you? / What happened to you?

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + object. Subject is omitted because context is obvious.
sentences.usageNotes: Can sound direct. Add 吧 or tone softening if needed.

examples.pinyin: gào sù wǒ fā shēng shén me le

sentences.pinyin: bú yào qiǎng wǒ de bèi zi
sentences.pinyinNumbered: bu2 yao4 qiang3 wo3 de5 bei4 zi5
sentences.structureNotes: 不要 + verb tells someone not to do something.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful playful couple phrase. 抢 means snatch/grab, not necessarily literally steal.

sentences.structureNotes: State + 了吗 asks whether someone has become that state.
sentences.usageNotes: Very common daily phrase.

sentences.structureNotes: No 'am' is needed. Mandarin can say 'I hungry now.'
sentences.usageNotes: Use when you became hungry or are hungry now.

sentences.english: What do you want to eat?
sentences.meaning: What do you want to eat?
sentences.structureNotes: Question words usually stay where the answer would go. Literally: you want eat what?
sentences.usageNotes: Essential food phrase.

examples.english: What do you want to drink?

sentences.hanzi: 今天晚上我们做什么饭？
sentences.pinyin: jīn tiān wǎn shang wǒ men zuò shén me fàn
sentences.pinyinNumbered: jin1 tian1 wan3 shang5 wo3 men5 zuo4 shen2 me5 fan4
sentences.literal: Today evening we make what meal?
sentences.structureNotes: Time phrase often comes at the beginning. 什么 stays where the unknown thing belongs.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural household dinner-planning sentence.

sentences.structureNotes: 在 before an action can show the action is happening now.
sentences.usageNotes: General phrase for cooking a meal.

sentences.structureNotes: 快...了 means something is about to happen or almost in a state.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural cooking phrase.

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + 一下 makes the action casual/brief.
sentences.usageNotes: Use when asking someone to try food.

sentences.structureNotes: Adjective/description + 吗 makes a yes/no question.
sentences.usageNotes: Use for food taste. Do not use 好 for food as naturally as 好吃.

sentences.structureNotes: 太 + adjective + 了 is a common reaction pattern.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful at meals.

sentences.structureNotes: No 'am' needed. State + 了 means the state is now true.
sentences.usageNotes: Common after eating.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ men qù mǎi cài ba
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 men5 qu4 mai3 cai4 ba5
sentences.structureNotes: 吧 often softens a suggestion, like 'let's...' or 'okay?'
sentences.usageNotes: 买菜 literally means buy vegetables, but commonly means grocery shopping/food shopping.

sentences.meaning: Do we need to buy anything?
sentences.pinyin: wǒ men xū yào mǎi shén me ma
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 men5 xu1 yao4 mai3 shen2 me5 ma5
sentences.structureNotes: 什么 can mean 'what' or 'anything' depending on context. This sentence is understandable and useful.
sentences.usageNotes: A more natural alternative: 还要买什么吗？= Do we still need to buy anything?

examples.pinyin: hái yào mǎi shén me ma
examples.english: Do we still need to buy anything?

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed greeting phrase.
sentences.usageNotes: 早 is also commonly used casually for morning greeting.

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + 得 + adjective describes how well an action was done.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural morning question. Treat 睡得好 as 'sleep well' chunk at first.

sentences.structureNotes: No 'am' needed. State + 了 means the state is now true.
sentences.usageNotes: One of the highest-frequency daily phrases.

sentences.structureNotes: 困 means sleepy, not maybe. 可能 means maybe.
sentences.usageNotes: Use when you are sleepy and want to sleep.

sentences.structureNotes: 我要 + action means I want/am going to do the action. 了 adds 'now/about to.'
sentences.usageNotes: Natural before going to bed.

examples.english: I am going to work now.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed greeting/goodbye phrase before sleep.
sentences.usageNotes: Common with partner/family/friends before bed.

sentences.structureNotes: Mandarin uses 关 for turning off lights/devices.
sentences.usageNotes: Short command. Add 一下 for softer request: 关一下灯.

sentences.structureNotes: Subject + need + object.
sentences.usageNotes: Straightforward and useful. 我想喝咖啡 means I want to drink coffee.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ de shǒu jī zài nǎ lǐ
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 de5 shou3 ji1 zai4 na3 li3
sentences.structureNotes: In Mandarin, the question word 哪里 stays where the location answer would appear.
sentences.usageNotes: Very useful daily phrase. 哪儿 can also mean where, especially in northern speech.

examples.pinyin: wǒ de yào shi zài nǎ lǐ

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + 一下 is a common soft request pattern.
sentences.usageNotes: Extremely common. 等等 is another casual option.

sentences.structureNotes: 帮我 means help me. 一下 makes the request softer.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful and natural for small help requests.

examples.pinyin: nǐ kě yǐ bāng wǒ ma

sentences.structureNotes: A short common phrase. Treat as a chunk first.
sentences.usageNotes: Use when you forgot something. 我忘记了 is also common.

sentences.structureNotes: Simple subject + verb phrase.
sentences.usageNotes: Common. Use 我不知道 for I don't know.

sentences.structureNotes: 不 goes before the verb to make it negative.
sentences.usageNotes: Very high-frequency phrase.

sentences.structureNotes: 我要 + action is a high-value beginner pattern.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural daily phrase.

sentences.structureNotes: 马上 before the verb means soon/immediately.
sentences.usageNotes: Common when stepping away briefly.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ jīn tiān yào shàng bān
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 jin1 tian1 yao4 shang4 ban1

breakdown.meaning: go to work / be at work

sentences.structureNotes: Time words often come early: subject + time + action.
sentences.usageNotes: 上班 means to work/go to work for a job shift, not generic study/work on a task.

examples.pinyin: wǒ jīn tiān bù shàng bān
examples.english: I do not have work today.

sentences.structureNotes: Location phrase 在家 comes before the main action 工作.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural for remote work.

examples.pinyin: wǒ zài gōng sī gōng zuò

sentences.structureNotes: 有 means have/there is.
sentences.usageNotes: 我的会议很无聊 = my meeting is boring.

examples.pinyin: wǒ xiàn zài yǒu huì yì

sentences.pinyin: wǒ de huì yì hěn wú liáo
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 de5 hui4 yi4 hen3 wu2 liao2
sentences.structureNotes: Subject + 很 + adjective. No explicit past tense is required if context is clear.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural work complaint.

examples.pinyin: zhè ge huì yì hěn wú liáo

sentences.structureNotes: 在 before an action can mark that the action is happening now.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful and relevant for programming work.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ de dài mǎ bù néng yùn xíng
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 de5 dai4 ma3 bu4 neng2 yun4 xing2
sentences.structureNotes: 不能 means cannot. Put it before the verb/action.
sentences.usageNotes: Natural enough for programming. More casual: 我的代码不行 = my code is no good/doesn't work.

examples.english: This doesn't work / this is not okay.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ zhǎo dào wèn tí le
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 zhao3 dao4 wen4 ti2 le5
sentences.structureNotes: 找到 emphasizes successfully finding something. 了 marks completion/change.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful for debugging.

sentences.structureNotes: Verb + 好 can mean the action was completed successfully.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful for code, devices, house stuff, etc.

sentences.pinyin: zhè ge méi yǒu dào lǐ
sentences.pinyinNumbered: zhe4 ge5 mei2 you3 dao4 li3
sentences.structureNotes: 没有 + noun means does not have that thing. Here: no logic/reason.
sentences.usageNotes: Understandable and useful. 这不合理 means this is unreasonable/illogical.

sentences.pinyin: ràng wǒ cè shì yí xià
sentences.pinyinNumbered: rang4 wo3 ce4 shi4 yi2 xia4
sentences.structureNotes: 让我 + action means let me do the action. 一下 softens the action.
sentences.usageNotes: Very useful dev phrase.

sentences.structureNotes: Use 我觉得 before your opinion. It is more natural than directly translating 'I think' with 想 in many opinion sentences.
sentences.usageNotes: High-value phrase for sounding like yourself.

examples.pinyin: wǒ jué de zhè ge hěn hǎo

sentences.pinyin: wǒ bù zhè me jué de
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 bu4 zhe4 me5 jue2 de5
sentences.structureNotes: This phrase literally says 'I don't think this way.'
sentences.usageNotes: Natural disagreement phrase, softer than saying someone is wrong.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed question word.
sentences.usageNotes: Extremely useful. Tone can sound curious or challenging.

examples.pinyin: nǐ wèi shén me shēng qì

sentences.structureNotes: Use 因为 before the reason.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful with 为什么.

sentences.structureNotes: 吧 makes the answer sound less blunt/certain.
sentences.usageNotes: This is the word you wanted for maybe. 困 means sleepy.

sentences.structureNotes: A common way to say someone is right. 对 alone can also mean correct/right.
sentences.usageNotes: You can also say 对, 你说得对 = Right, what you said is correct.

sentences.structureNotes: Short fixed phrase for admitting fault or being incorrect.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful in relationships and conversations.

examples.pinyin: duì bù qǐ, wǒ cuò le

sentences.structureNotes: Subject + 很 + adjective.
sentences.usageNotes: Can be insulting depending on context. Use carefully.

sentences.pinyin: zhè ge hěn yǒu yì si
sentences.pinyinNumbered: zhe4 ge5 hen3 you3 yi4 si5
sentences.structureNotes: 有意思 literally has meaning/interest, but functions as interesting.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful positive reaction phrase.

sentences.pinyin: wǒ yǒu yí ge xiǎng fǎ
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 you3 yi2 ge5 xiang3 fa3
sentences.structureNotes: 有 means have. 一个 is a general 'a/an/one' with the common measure word 个.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful for projects and conversations.

examples.pinyin: zhè shì yí ge hǎo xiǎng fǎ

sentences.structureNotes: 不 goes before the verb/adjective to negate it.
sentences.usageNotes: Very important beginner phrase.

examples.english: I don't understand what I hear.

sentences.structureNotes: 再 before the verb means do it again.
sentences.usageNotes: Critical phrase for learning from native speakers. Audio uses forced tone marks so 一遍 is pronounced biàn, not misread as 四 (sì).

sentences.structureNotes: Adjective + 一点 means a little more adjective. 慢一点 = a little slower.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful with partner/family/native speakers.

sentences.english: How do you say this in Chinese?
sentences.meaning: How do you say this in Chinese?
sentences.pinyin: zhè ge zhōng wén zěn me shuō
sentences.pinyinNumbered: zhe4 ge5 zhong1 wen2 zen3 me5 shuo1
sentences.structureNotes: 怎么 + verb means how to do the verb. 中文怎么说 = how is it said in Chinese?
sentences.usageNotes: One of the most important meta-learning phrases.

examples.pinyin: zhè ge yīng wén zěn me shuō
examples.english: How do you say this in English?

sentences.pinyin: nǐ kě yǐ jiāo wǒ ma
sentences.pinyinNumbered: ni3 ke3 yi3 jiao1 wo3 ma5
sentences.structureNotes: 你可以 + action + 吗 asks whether someone can do something.
sentences.usageNotes: Good phrase for asking your girlfriend for help.

examples.pinyin: nǐ kě yǐ bāng wǒ ma

sentences.pinyin: zhè shì shén me yì si
sentences.pinyinNumbered: zhe4 shi4 shen2 me5 yi4 si5
sentences.structureNotes: 什么 stays where the unknown answer would be. Literally: this is what meaning?
sentences.usageNotes: Critical for learning new words/phrases.

examples.pinyin: zhè ge cí shì shén me yì si

sentences.meaning: I am going to buy groceries.
sentences.structureNotes: 要去 + verb means going to go do an action.
sentences.usageNotes: Very useful household phrase.

examples.english: I am going to the store.

sentences.structureNotes: 太 + adjective + 了 is a common reaction pattern.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful anywhere money/cost comes up.

sentences.structureNotes: 不 goes before 需要 to negate it.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful for shopping or refusing offers.

sentences.structureNotes: 我想 + verb gives a softer desire/want.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful in stores and online shopping talk.

sentences.structureNotes: Time words often appear before or after the subject, before the main action.
sentences.usageNotes: Core time word.

sentences.structureNotes: Core time word.
sentences.usageNotes: Use in future planning.

sentences.structureNotes: 等一下 can mean wait a moment or in a little bit depending on context.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful for procrastination and daily tasks.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed goodbye phrase.
sentences.usageNotes: Common when you will see someone tomorrow.

sentences.structureNotes: Basic greeting. Two third tones together often make the first sound like second tone in speech.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful but less common with close people than casual greetings like 早 or direct conversation.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed polite phrase.
sentences.usageNotes: Common and useful.

sentences.structureNotes: Fixed apology phrase.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful but can sound heavier than 不好意思 depending on situation.

sentences.structureNotes: 没 is a shortened form of 没有 in many phrases.
sentences.usageNotes: Common positive reply.

sentences.english: I only speak a little Chinese.
sentences.meaning: I only speak a little Chinese.
sentences.pinyin: wǒ zhǐ huì shuō yì diǎn zhōng wén
sentences.pinyinNumbered: wo3 zhi3 hui4 shuo1 yi4 dian3 zhong1 wen2
sentences.literal: I only can speak a little Chinese.
sentences.structureNotes: 只会 means only know how to/can. 一点 means a little.
sentences.usageNotes: Great real-world phrase for beginner interactions.

examples.pinyin: wǒ huì shuō yì diǎn zhōng wén
examples.english: I can speak a little Chinese.

sentences.structureNotes: 在 + verb can mean currently doing the verb.
sentences.usageNotes: Useful when explaining your learning effort.

dailyMinimum: Use at least 3 Mandarin sentences in real life or aloud.
